---
title: "linear regression"
format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
jupyter: python3
---

$y_i = \beta_0 + \beta_1 x_i + \epsilon_i$ on crude oil.

```{python}
import yfinance as yf
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import datetime as dt
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression
from sklearn.metrics import r2_score

# 1. Download dati
ticker = "CL=F"
df = yf.download(ticker, start="2020-01-01", end="2026-03-15")
df = df[['Close']].dropna().reset_index()

# 2. Pre-processing
# Trasformiamo le date in numeri (ordinali) perché i modelli lineari richiedono input numerici
X = df['Date'].map(dt.datetime.toordinal).values.reshape(-1, 1)
y = df['Close'].values

# 3. Fit del Modello
model = LinearRegression()
model.fit(X, y)

# Calcolo accuratezza sul passato (R^2)
r2 = r2_score(y, model.predict(X))

# 4. Proiezione fino al 31 Dicembre 2027
future_dates = pd.date_range(start="2026-03-16", end="2027-12-31", freq='D')
X_future = future_dates.map(dt.datetime.toordinal).values.reshape(-1, 1)
y_future = model.predict(X_future)

# 5. Visualizzazione
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
plt.scatter(df['Date'], y, color='lightgray', s=10, label='Dati reali (Daily)')
plt.plot(df['Date'], model.predict(X), color='red', linewidth=2, label='Trend Lineare (Training)')
plt.plot(future_dates, y_future, color='green', linestyle='--', linewidth=2, label='Proiezione 2027')

plt.title(f"Previsione Lineare WTI (R² = {r2:.4f})")
plt.ylabel("Prezzo USD")
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
plt.show()

print(f"Equazione del trend: Prezzo = {float(model.coef_[0]):.6f} * Giorno + {float(model.intercept_):.2f}")
```
